# backup

```go
import "github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/internal/backup"
```

Package backup implements snapshot creation, storage, and retrieval for USB CRD backup and restore workflows.

<a name="MarshalSnapshot"></a>
## func [MarshalSnapshot](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/snapshot.go#L67>)

```go
func MarshalSnapshot(s *Snapshot) ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalSnapshot serializes a Snapshot to JSON bytes.

Intent: Produce the byte payload for storage backends. Inputs: A valid Snapshot. Outputs: JSON byte representation. Errors: Returns a JSON marshalling error.

<a name="ValidateChecksum"></a>
## func [ValidateChecksum](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/snapshot.go#L91>)

```go
func ValidateChecksum(s *Snapshot) (bool, error)
```

ValidateChecksum verifies that a Snapshot's data matches its stored checksum.

Intent: Detect corruption or tampering in backup data. Inputs: A Snapshot to validate. Outputs: True when the computed checksum matches the stored value. Errors: Returns false with no additional error detail.

<a name="BackupMetadata"></a>
## type [BackupMetadata](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/storage.go#L14-L17>)

BackupMetadata describes a stored backup without its data payload. Used by BackupStorage.List to enumerate available snapshots.

```go
type BackupMetadata struct {
    Name string
    Size int64
}
```

<a name="BackupStorage"></a>
## type [BackupStorage](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/storage.go#L26-L31>)

BackupStorage abstracts how backup snapshots are persisted and retrieved. Implementations include ConfigMap, PVC, and S3 backends selected at runtime via NewStorage based on USBBackupConfig destination settings.

@component Storage\["Backup Storage"\] \-\-\> CMStorage\["ConfigMapStorage"\] @component Storage\["Backup Storage"\] \-\-\> PVCBackupStorage\["PVCStorage"\] @component Storage\["Backup Storage"\] \-\-\> S3BackupStorage\["S3Storage"\]

```go
type BackupStorage interface {
    Write(ctx context.Context, name string, data []byte) error
    Read(ctx context.Context, name string) ([]byte, error)
    List(ctx context.Context) ([]BackupMetadata, error)
    Delete(ctx context.Context, name string) error
}
```

<a name="NewStorage"></a>
### func [NewStorage](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/storage.go#L40>)

```go
func NewStorage(dest usbv1alpha1.BackupDestination) (BackupStorage, error)
```

NewStorage returns a BackupStorage implementation based on the destination type configured in the USBBackupConfig spec.

Intent: Decouple callers from concrete storage backends. Inputs: Destination configuration from USBBackupConfigSpec. Outputs: BackupStorage implementation for the configured backend. Errors: Returns an error for unsupported or misconfigured destination types.

<a name="ConfigMapStorage"></a>
## type [ConfigMapStorage](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/configmap_storage.go#L16-L20>)

ConfigMapStorage is an in\-memory backup storage implementation that models Kubernetes ConfigMap semantics. In production, this would interact with the Kubernetes API; the current implementation uses a map for unit\-test and small\-deployment use.

@component CMStorage\["ConfigMapStorage"\] \-\-\> K8sAPI\["Kubernetes API"\]

```go
type ConfigMapStorage struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```


NewConfigMapStorage returns a ConfigMapStorage backed by the named ConfigMap.

<a name="PVCStorage"></a>
## type [PVCStorage](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/pvc_storage.go#L17-L20>)

PVCStorage persists backup snapshots as files in a PVC\-mounted directory. Each snapshot is stored as a JSON file; directory structure is derived from the PVC claim name and optional sub\-path.

@component PVCBackupStorage\["PVCStorage"\] \-\-\> Filesystem\["PVC Mount"\]

```go
type PVCStorage struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewPVCStorage"></a>

```go
func NewPVCStorage(claimName, subPath string) *PVCStorage
```

NewPVCStorage returns a PVCStorage rooted at the PVC mount path and optional sub\-path.

<a name="NewPVCStorageWithPath"></a>
### func [NewPVCStorageWithPath](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/pvc_storage.go#L33>)

```go
func NewPVCStorageWithPath(basePath string) *PVCStorage
```

NewPVCStorageWithPath returns a PVCStorage with an explicit base path. Useful for testing without a real PVC mount.

<a name="S3Storage"></a>
## type [S3Storage](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/s3_storage.go#L15-L21>)

S3Storage is a placeholder implementation for S3\-compatible backup storage. A production implementation would use an S3 SDK; this stub stores data in memory so the interface contract is exercised in tests.

@component S3BackupStorage\["S3Storage"\] \-\-\> S3Bucket\["S3 Endpoint"\]

```go
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Snapshot"></a>
## type [Snapshot](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/snapshot.go#L26-L31>)

Snapshot is the top\-level envelope written to backup storage. It wraps the SnapshotData with metadata \(version, timestamp\) and a SHA\-256 checksum for integrity verification during restore.

```go
type Snapshot struct {
    Version   string       `json:"version"`
    CreatedAt time.Time    `json:"createdAt"`
    Checksum  string       `json:"checksum"`
    Data      SnapshotData `json:"data"`
}
```

<a name="CreateSnapshot"></a>
### func [CreateSnapshot](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/snapshot.go#L39>)

```go
func CreateSnapshot(whitelists []usbv1alpha1.USBDeviceWhitelist, policies []usbv1alpha1.USBDevicePolicy, approvals []usbv1alpha1.USBDeviceApproval) (*Snapshot, error)
```

CreateSnapshot builds a Snapshot from the provided resource lists.

Intent: Produce a self\-describing, checksummed backup payload. Inputs: Whitelists, policies, and approvals to include. Outputs: A Snapshot with computed checksum. Errors: Returns a JSON marshalling error if any resource is not serializable.

<a name="ReadSnapshot"></a>
### func [ReadSnapshot](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/snapshot.go#L127>)

```go
func ReadSnapshot(ctx context.Context, storage BackupStorage, name string) (*Snapshot, error)
```

ReadSnapshot reads and validates a snapshot from storage.

Intent: Single\-step backup retrieval with integrity check. Inputs: Context, storage backend, and backup name. Outputs: The validated Snapshot. Errors: Returns storage read, unmarshal, or checksum validation errors.

<a name="UnmarshalSnapshot"></a>
### func [UnmarshalSnapshot](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/snapshot.go#L77>)

```go
func UnmarshalSnapshot(data []byte) (*Snapshot, error)
```

UnmarshalSnapshot deserializes JSON bytes into a Snapshot.

Intent: Reconstruct a Snapshot from stored data. Inputs: JSON bytes. Outputs: Parsed Snapshot struct. Errors: Returns a JSON unmarshalling error.

<a name="WriteSnapshot"></a>
### func [WriteSnapshot](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/snapshot.go#L106>)

```go
func WriteSnapshot(ctx context.Context, storage BackupStorage, name string, whitelists []usbv1alpha1.USBDeviceWhitelist, policies []usbv1alpha1.USBDevicePolicy, approvals []usbv1alpha1.USBDeviceApproval) (*Snapshot, error)
```

WriteSnapshot creates a snapshot and writes it to storage.

Intent: Single\-step backup creation for reconciler use. Inputs: Context, storage backend, backup name, and resources to back up. Outputs: The created Snapshot. Errors: Returns snapshot creation or storage write errors.

<a name="SnapshotData"></a>
## type [SnapshotData](<https://github.com/grethel-labs/kubelink-usb/blob/main/internal/backup/snapshot.go#L17-L21>)

SnapshotData holds the serializable configuration resources captured in a backup. It contains all security\-relevant CRs that define the system's policy state: whitelists \(fingerprint\-based auto\-approve\), policies \(selector rules\), and approvals \(individual device decisions\).

```go
type SnapshotData struct {
    Whitelists []usbv1alpha1.USBDeviceWhitelist `json:"whitelists"`
    Policies   []usbv1alpha1.USBDevicePolicy    `json:"policies"`
    Approvals  []usbv1alpha1.USBDeviceApproval  `json:"approvals"`
}
```
